specification.topic-statement: The agent kill tool terminates a single running agent by UUID, updating its status in the database and synchronizing the change to in-memory state.

scope.in-scope: Validation of the kill request, the database status transition from running to dead, deferred in-memory state update, and the JSON result produced for the calling LLM.
scope.boundary: The UUID of the target agent is extracted from a JSON argument string. This topic receives the extracted string.

This topic queries whether an agent exists and what its current status and parent UUID are. It also issues the status transition that marks an agent dead. The database operations are the authoritative source for agent state.
The raw result or error produced by this topic is wrapped in an envelope format before being returned to the tool execution infrastructure.

After the database transition completes, a deferred callback updates the in-memory agent list held by the REPL. This topic deposits data for the callback to consume; the REPL itself is not accessed during the main execution phase.

field.name: uuid
field.note: UUID of the agent to terminate

field.name: code
field.note: one of: PARSE_ERROR, INVALID_ARG, AGENT_NOT_FOUND, ALREADY_DEAD, CANNOT_KILL_ROOT, CANNOT_KILL_PARENT, DB_ERROR

behavior.argument-parsing:
The JSON argument string is parsed. If parsing fails, the tool returns an error with code PARSE_ERROR and does not proceed.
The parsed object must contain a field named "uuid" whose value is a string. If the field is absent or not a string, the tool returns an error with code INVALID_ARG.
behavior.target-validation:
The target agent is looked up by UUID in the database. If no matching agent is found, the tool returns an error with code AGENT_NOT_FOUND.
If the agent exists but its status is not "running", the tool returns an error with code ALREADY_DEAD. No status transition is performed.
If the target agent has no parent (identifying it as the root agent), the tool returns an error with code CANNOT_KILL_ROOT. The root agent is never killable.
behavior.caller-validation:
The calling agent's own record is fetched from the database. If this lookup fails, the tool returns an error with code DB_ERROR.
If the calling agent's parent UUID matches the target UUID — meaning the calling agent is attempting to kill its own parent — the tool returns an error with code CANNOT_KILL_PARENT.
behavior.status-transition:
The target agent's status is changed from "running" to "dead" in the database, and its end timestamp is recorded. This operation only affects rows whose current status is "running", making it a no-op if the agent was already dead by the time the update executes.
If the database update fails, the tool returns an error with code DB_ERROR.
behavior.deferred-memory-update:
After the database transition succeeds, the target UUID is stored for processing by a deferred callback that runs on the main thread after the tool handler returns. That callback scans the REPL's in-memory agent list and sets the matching entry's dead flag to true.
If the target UUID does not appear in the REPL's in-memory agent list (possible if the agent was created in a different process or session), the deferred callback completes without error and without updating any in-memory entry.
behavior.success-result:
On success, the tool returns a JSON object containing a "killed" field whose value is an array containing the target UUID as its sole element.
Only the directly specified agent is terminated. Descendant agents are not killed. The "killed" array always contains exactly one UUID, regardless of how many descendants the target has.

state-transitions.transition: Target agent status changes from "running" to "dead"; end timestamp recorded
Matching in-memory agent entry's dead flag set to true
